channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than 2 Decades
item.description: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years.

item.title: UAE Tells OPEC Its Oil Production Surged Last Month
item.description: The United Arab Emirates informed OPEC that its oil production surged by 80 percent last month.

item.title: USA Strategic Petroleum Reserve is 56 Percent Empty
item.description: Or 44 percent full - depending on how you want to look at it.

item.title: South Africa Plans First Oil Reserves Boost Since Apartheid
item.description: South Africa plans to increase its strategic oil reserves for the first time since the apartheid government stockpiled crude.

item.title: IEA Chief Slams Europe’s Slow Electrification as Energy Crisis Deepens
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency has warned Europe made a “major mistake” by failing to end its economy reliance on imported fossil fuels quickly enough since the 2022 energy crunch. Fatih Birol said Europe’s…

item.title: Falling Fertility Threatens Energy Demand, WoodMac Warns
item.description: Falling fertility rates threaten long term energy demand, Wood Mackenzie warned.

item.title: Utility-Scale Solar Costs Rise 18% but Remain Cheapest Power Source
item.description: The cost of unsubsidized utility-scale solar rose by 18% to $40-$98/MWh, driven by higher capital costs, interest rates, tariffs, and supply chain pressures, a Lazard's Levelized Cost of Energy+ analysis has found…

item.title: Oil prices rise more than 3% amid new US-Iran military strikes near Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices climbed by more than 3% on 13 July, Monday, following renewed military exchanges between the US and Iran, prompting concerns over the security of global energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Jumps After Battle for Hormuz Control Escalates
item.description: 'Fresh strikes by U.S. and Iran renewed concerns about the safe passage of oil and other key commodities through the narrow waterway', Saxo Bank said.

item.title: ADNOC L&S orders four LNG carriers from Jiangnan for $900m
item.description: ADNOC Logistics and Services (L&S), a subsidiary of ADNOC, has signed a contract worth approximately $900m (Dh3.3bn) with Jiangnan Shipyard for the construction of four new liquefied natural gas (LNG) carriers.

item.title: EU's Russian LNG Imports Hit Record High Ahead of 2027 Ban
item.description: The European Union imported a record 9.97 million metric tons of liquefied natural gas (LNG) worth €5.96 billion (~$6.82 billion) from Russia's Yamal LNG facility in the first half of 2026, marking a 16% increase…

item.title: Why Renewed Iran Tensions Could Keep Fuel Prices Elevated
item.description: The oil market has officially entered its mixed-signals era. Crude prices had erased most of their wartime gains as barrels returned to the market and fears of oversupply resurfaced. But renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities…

item.title: Global Emissions Hit Another Record High Despite Clean Energy Boom
item.description: The Energy Institute has released the 2026 Statistical Review of World Energy, published in partnership with Ember and in collaboration with KPMG and Kearney. The Statistical Review, previously published for more than…

item.title: USA, Iran Trade Fresh Strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday.

item.title: Masdar Secures $5.1 Billion for World’s Largest Solar-and-Battery Project
item.description: Masdar on Monday announced it had lined up financing for the world’s first gigascale Round-the-Clock renewable energy project, which the renewable energy giant of the United Arab Emirates is developing in Abu Dhabi. The…

item.title: EC conditionally approves $13.6bn Baker Hughes-Chart deal
item.description: The European Commission (EC) has conditionally approved the proposed $13.6bn acquisition of Chart Industries by Baker Hughes under the EU Merger Regulation.

item.title: France Cuts 6.4 GW of Nuclear Power as Heatwave Grips the Country
item.description: France’s nuclear power generation was slashed by 6.4 gigawatts (GW) on Monday amid a prolonged and intense heatwave that hiked river temperatures and limited the ability of the power plants to use the water to cool…

item.title: Nigeria’s Oil Production Hits Six-Year High as Middle East Risks Return
item.description: Nigeria’s crude oil production hit a six-year high in June, as operations continued to stabilize and no major outage incidents occurred, just as the new flare-up in Iran threatens flows through the Strait of Hormuz…

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Tanker Traffic Falls to Five-Week Low
item.description: Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz dipped on Sunday to the lowest number in five weeks as the latest U.S.-Iran escalation reignited concerns among ship operators about safety at the key oil and LNG chokepoint…

item.title: Iran Warns U.S. Interference Could Trigger Further Oil and Gas Disruptions
item.description: Following a major U.S.-Iran re-escalation over the Strait of Hormuz this weekend, Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) on Monday warned that continued U.S. interference in the chokepoint “could lead to…
